arguments.content:
# API Integration Test Plan

## Document Information
- **Version:** v2.3.0
- **Created:** April 6, 2026
- **Author:** Backend Engineering Team
- **Review Cycle:** Per Sprint

---

## Test Environment Setup

### Prerequisites
- Node.js v18.x or higher
- Docker and Docker Compose
- PostgreSQL 15 (for test database)
- Redis 7.x (for rate limiting tests)

### Environment Configuration

```bash
# Test environment variables
export NODE_ENV=test
export API_BASE_URL=https://api-staging.metaclaw.io/v2
export TEST_DB_URL=postgresql://test_user:test_pass@localhost:5432/metaclaw_test
export TEST_API_TOKEN=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
export RATE_LIMIT_TEST_ENABLED=true
```

### Database Setup
```bash
# Initialize test database
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d
npm run db:migrate:test
npm run db:seed:test
```

### Test Data Fixtures
- **Test Users:** 5 pre-configured test users with various roles
- **Test Projects:** 3 sample projects with different statuses
- **Test Tasks:** 15 tasks associated with test projects

---

## Test Case List

### TC-001: Authentication - Valid Token
**Objective:** Verify API accepts valid Bearer tokens

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Send GET /users with valid token | 200 OK response |
| 2 | Verify response contains user data | Data returned with correct schema |

### TC-002: Authentication - Invalid Token
**Objective:** Verify API rejects invalid/missing tokens

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Send GET /users without Authorization header | 401 Unauthorized |
| 2 | Send GET /users with malformed token | 401 Unauthorized |
| 3 | Send GET /users with expired token | 401 Unauthorized |

### TC-003: Users Endpoint - CRUD Operations
**Objective:** Verify complete user lifecycle

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | POST /users with valid data | 201 Created, user returned |
| 2 | GET /users/{id} for created user | 200 OK, user data matches |
| 3 | PUT /users/{id} with updates | 200 OK, changes persisted |
| 4 | DELETE /users/{id} | 204 No Content |
| 5 | GET /users/{id} (deleted) | 404 Not Found |

### TC-004: Projects Endpoint - Create Project
**Objective:** Verify project creation with valid data

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | POST /projects with all required fields | 201 Created |
| 2 | Verify response contains project ID | ID format: prj_xxx |
| 3 | Verify default status is "draft" | status field = "draft" |

### TC-005: Projects Endpoint - Validation Errors
**Objective:** Verify input validation on project creation

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | POST /projects without name field | 400 Bad Request, field error |
| 2 | POST /projects with name > 100 chars | 400 Bad Request, length error |
| 3 | POST /projects with invalid owner ID | 404 Not Found |

### TC-006: Projects Endpoint - List with Filters
**Objective:** Verify project listing and filtering

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | GET /projects | Returns all accessible projects |
| 2 | GET /projects?status=active | Only active projects |
| 3 | GET /projects?owner=usr_abc123 | Only projects by owner |
| 4 | GET /projects with pagination | Correct page/limit applied |

### TC-007: Tasks Endpoint - Assignment Flow
**Objective:** Verify task assignment to users

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Create task via POST /tasks | 201 Created |
| 2 | Assign task via PUT /tasks/{id} | 200 OK, assignee updated |
| 3 | Verify task appears in user's list | GET /tasks?assignee={id} |

### TC-008: Rate Limiting
**Objective:** Verify rate limiting enforcement

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Send 110 requests within 1 hour (Free tier) | First 100 succeed, 10 return 429 |
| 2 | Verify rate limit headers present | X-RateLimit-* headers |
| 3 | After reset window, requests succeed | 200 OK responses |

### TC-009: Error Response Format
**Objective:** Verify consistent error response structure

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Trigger various error conditions | All return JSON error object |
| 2 | Verify error object schema | Contains code, message, details |
| 3 | Verify HTTP status codes | Correct status for each error type |

### TC-010: Concurrent Access
**Objective:** Verify API handles concurrent requests

| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Send 50 concurrent GET /users | All return 200 OK |
| 2 | Send 20 concurrent POST /projects | All create successfully |
| 3 | Verify no duplicate or lost records | Database state consistent |

---

## Expected Results Summary

### Success Criteria
- All test cases must pass with expected HTTP status codes
- Response times for all endpoints must be < 200ms (p95)
- No data integrity issues after concurrent operations
- Rate limiting enforced correctly across all tiers

### Test Coverage Requirements
| Component | Minimum Coverage |
|-----------|------------------|
| User endpoints | 100% (all CRUD operations) |
| Project endpoints | 100% (all CRUD operations) |
| Task endpoints | 100% (all CRUD operations) |
| Authentication | 100% (all token scenarios) |
| Error handling | 100% (all error codes) |
| Rate limiting | 100% (all tier scenarios) |

### Performance Benchmarks
| Endpoint | p50 Latency | p95 Latency | p99 Latency |
|----------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| GET /users | < 50ms | < 100ms | < 200ms |
| POST /projects | < 80ms | < 150ms | < 250ms |
| GET /projects | < 60ms | < 120ms | < 200ms |
| PUT /tasks/{id} | < 70ms | < 130ms | < 220ms |

### Known Limitations
- File upload tests excluded (pending storage service integration)
- Webhook delivery tests require external endpoint setup
- Load testing (>1000 concurrent) requires dedicated environment

---

## Test Execution Commands

```bash
# Run all integration tests
npm run test:integration

# Run specific test suite
npm run test:integration -- --grep "Projects"

# Run with coverage report
npm run test:integration:coverage

# Run in CI mode (headless)
npm run test:integration:ci
```

## Sign-off

| Role | Name | Date | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Test Lead | | | |
| Backend Lead | Alex Zhang | | |
| QA Engineer | Dana | | |

---

**Last Updated:** April 6, 2026

✅ **API Integration Test Document Created**

I've created `day16/api_integration_tests.md` with the following structure:

- **Test Environment Setup** — Prerequisites, environment configuration, database setup instructions, and test data fixtures
- **Test Case List** — 10 comprehensive test cases covering:
  - Authentication (valid/invalid tokens)
  - Users endpoint CRUD operations
  - Projects endpoint (creation, validation, filtering)
  - Tasks assignment flow
  - Rate limiting enforcement
  - Error response consistency
  - Concurrent access handling
- **Expected Results** — Success criteria, coverage requirements, performance benchmarks (p50/p95/p99 latencies), and test execution commands

The document follows the team's professional documentation standards and integrates with the existing Sprint 9 materials.
